name: Wake County Public School System
about: Wake County Public School System serves approximately 161,000 students in the Raleigh-Durham metropolitan area and is the largest school district in North Carolina.
calendarNotes: Wake County Public School System typically starts the school year in late August and includes a Fall Break in mid-October — a calendar feature common across North Carolina but less familiar to families moving from other states. Spring Break usually falls in mid-to-late March. Wake County is one of the fastest-growing school districts in the country, driven by the Research Triangle's technology and life sciences economy, and attendance boundaries are periodically adjusted to accommodate new development. Families new to the Raleigh-Durham area should confirm their assigned school before assuming based on address. The district's large size means some communities are well-served by neighborhood schools while others rely on district transportation.
